ELPAC Level 4
NWEA 1300–1400L
DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS12-L4-13001400-HSELA1112-MMG-CFI-01 • HS English 11-12 • Promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making

The Two Quiet Rules

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a teacher and a student. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What are the teacher and student discussing?
2QUESTION 2: What does the teacher say about the silence rule?
3QUESTION 3: What is the student most likely to do next based on this conversation?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Build a three-part justification. Identify the fusion the procedure represents, the function the silence rule actually performs, and the revision the teacher directs. Support each with a specific moment from the dialogue, and explain how that moment makes the answer the right one.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.6  |  CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.SL.11-12.1  |  HS English 11-12: Promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making
